name: Gorbeia and Aldamin Loop
name_original: Gorbeia y Aldamiñape — circular por el Parque Natural de Gorbeia
description: Hike the moderate 5.3-mile Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular trail in Gorbeia Natural Park, gaining 1958 feet over 3 hours and 40 minutes.
region_intro: Hiking around Zeanuri offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Gorbeia Natural Park in the Basque Country. The region features rugged topography with prominent peaks such as Mount Gorbea, Aldamin, and Lekanda. Lush natural forests, primarily composed of beech trees, cover much of the terrain, alongside extensive natural pastures. Key natural features include the Saldropo wetland, the Otzarreta beech forest, and the Uguna waterfall, all contributing to a varied hiking environment. Best hiking trails around Zeanuri The most popular hiking route is Axkorrigan, Ojo de Atxular y cueva de Superlegor — loop through the Protected Biotope of Itxina, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete, traversing the Protected Biotope of Itxina. Another top favourite among local hikers is Humedal de Saldropo y Cascada Uguna — circular por el Parque Natural de Gorbeia, an easy 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This circular route explores the Saldropo wetland and Uguna waterfall within Gorbeia Natural Park. Local hikers also love the Gorbeia y Aldamiñape — circular by the Gorbeia Natural Park, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through Gorbeia Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Zeanuri is defined by mountain ranges, dense beech forests, and wetlands, offering a network of routes suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Zeanuri are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 170 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Zeanuri's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Zeanuri?
A: Zeanuri offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 55 trails available. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging mountain treks within the Gorbeia Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking options around Zeanuri?
A: Yes, Zeanuri provides several easy hiking options. There are 12 routes classified as easy, perfect for a relaxed walk. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035899066/humedal-de-saldropo-y-cascada-uguna-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-gorbeia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humedal de Saldropo y Cascada Uguna — circular por el Parque Natural de Gorbeia&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.5-mile (5.6 km) path that explores the Saldropo wetland and Uguna waterfall.

Q: Can I find hikes that feature waterfalls in Zeanuri?
A: Absolutely. The Gorbeia Natural Park, near Zeanuri, is home to the picturesque Uguna waterfall. You can experience this natural beauty on the circular route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035899066/humedal-de-saldropo-y-cascada-uguna-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-gorbeia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humedal de Saldropo y Cascada Uguna&lt;/a&gt;, which also takes you through the Saldropo wetland.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Zeanuri?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Zeanuri are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035966575/arralde-y-atxuri-through-the-otzarreta-beech-forest-circular-route-in-the-gorbeia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arralde y Atxuri through the Otzarreta beech forest&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route that showcases the serene beauty of the ancient beech forest.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Zeanuri?
A: Hiking in Zeanuri offers diverse natural features within the Gorbeia Natural Park. You'll encounter rugged mountain ranges, lush beech forests like the Otzarreta, and unique wetlands such as Saldropo. The area is also characterized by prominent peaks like Mount Gorbea and the scenic Uguna waterfall.

Q: Are there any unique attractions or landmarks to visit near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, Zeanuri and its surroundings offer several interesting attractions. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/628313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiola Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, explore the restored Olabarri Mill, or discover the inactive San Justo Limestone Quarry. For those interested in mountain peaks, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3876692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross&lt;/a&gt; is a significant landmark.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Zeanuri?
A: The hiking trails in Zeanuri are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the stunning natural beauty of the Gorbeia Natural Park.

Q: Can I explore the famous Otzarreta Beech Forest on a hike?
A: Yes, the enchanting Otzarreta Beech Forest is a highlight of the region and can be explored on several hikes. A popular option is the circular route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035966575/arralde-y-atxuri-through-the-otzarreta-beech-forest-circular-route-in-the-gorbeia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arralde y Atxuri through the Otzarreta beech forest&lt;/a&gt;, offering a deep dive into its serene beauty.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Zeanuri?
A: Zeanuri is an excellent destination for experienced trekkers, with 21 routes classified as difficult. These trails often lead to higher elevations and offer more rugged terrain. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035939734/axkorrigan-ojo-de-atxular-y-cueva-de-superlegor-loop-through-the-protected-biotope-of-itxina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Axkorrigan, Ojo de Atxular y cueva de Superlegor&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging loop through the Protected Biotope of Itxina.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Zeanuri?
A: The Gorbeia region experiences a humid and rainy mountain climate, often shrouded in fog. While hiking is possible year-round, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery with lush greenery or autumnal colors. Summers are generally mild, but be prepared for potential rain and fog at any time.

Q: Are hiking trails in Zeanuri dog-friendly?
A: Many trails within the Gorbeia Natural Park are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural park areas, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trails in Zeanuri?
A: While Zeanuri itself is accessible by public transport, direct public transport links to specific trailheads within the Gorbeia Natural Park can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to access the starting points of many popular routes.

Q: What are the options for parking when hiking in Zeanuri?
A: Parking is generally available at or near the main trailheads and popular starting points for hikes in Zeanuri and the Gorbeia Natural Park. Look for designated parking areas, especially near visitor centers or recreational zones like Saldropo, to ensure convenient access to the trails.
tips: The Egiriñao beech forest, where the refuge of the same name is located, stands out for its frequent mists that create a magical, legendary atmosphere.

faqs.question: How long does the Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular hike take?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular hike typically takes around 3 hours and 41 minutes to complete. This estimate is for continuous movement and does not include breaks for enjoying the scenery or resting.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain, including tracks and paths that are mostly accessible. You'll encounter areas with steep rock faces, gentle rolling pastures, and extensive beech and oak forests. Sure-footedness is required, especially in sections of the karst landscape with its unique formations.

faqs.question: What landmarks and viewpoints can I see along the Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass several notable points of interest. These include the serene Egiriñao Beech Forest and the historic Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de las Nieves (Egiriñao). You'll also reach the Gorbea Summit and the Aldamiñape Summit Monument (1,050 m), offering spectacular views of the Gorbeia Natural Park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not detailed, but the Gorbeia Natural Park has several fully-equipped recreational areas and visitor centers in Sarria (Álava) and Areatza (Bizkaia) which often serve as starting points with parking facilities. It's advisable to check local park information for the most convenient access point for this specific circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in Gorbeia Natural Park, but it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please be mindful of local regulations regarding pets within the natural park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular trail?
faqs.answer: The Gorbeia Natural Park offers beautiful scenery year-round. However, for the most comfortable hiking experience, spring and autumn are often ideal, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring snow to higher elevations, requiring appropriate gear and caution.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for hiking in Gorbeia Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required for general hiking activities within Gorbeia Natural Park. However, it's always a good idea to check the official Gorbeia Natural Park website or visitor centers for any updated regulations or specific activity requirements.

faqs.question: Does this circular route connect with other trails in Gorbeia Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Bizkaiko bira, Artzaintza naturbidea, and Euskal Herriko ibilaldia, among others. This offers opportunities to explore further or combine parts of different routes.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: This hike showcases the diverse ecosystems of Gorbeia Natural Park. You'll experience dense woods of beech, Pyrenean oak, and chestnut trees, open grazing lands with heather, and unique karst landscapes featuring sinkholes and rugged limestone cliffs. The scenery is considered beautiful and spectacular throughout.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular hike?
faqs.answer: The Gorbeia y Aldamiñape circular hike is classified as moderate. It involves an elevation gain of approximately 600 meters and requires a good level of fitness. While paths are mostly accessible, sure-footedness is necessary due to varied terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or visitor centers near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gorbeia Natural Park provides visitor centers in Sarria (Álava) and Areatza (Bizkaia). These centers offer information about the park, its trails, and local services, and can be a good resource before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de las Nieves (Egiriñao)
